messages.content:
I can’t run tasks from here because I don’t have access to your execution environment. (I can, however, tell you exactly how to run it and how to interpret results.)

Quick questions so I give precise commands:
- Which tool/script implements “load test task 1” in this repo? (k6, locust, JMeter, Artillery, Gatling, a Makefile/npm script, or something else?)
- Where is the test file or the command you normally use (path or package.json script name)?

If you don’t know, try one of these common commands depending on the tool your project uses:

- k6
  - Run: k6 run tests/task1.js
  - Docker: docker run -i --rm -v "$(pwd)":/scripts -w /scripts loadimpact/k6 run tests/task1.js

- Locust (headless)
  - locust -f locustfile.py --headless -u 100 -r 10 -t 1m --host http://your-target
  - Adjust -u (users), -r (spawn rate), -t (duration)

- JMeter (CLI)
  - jmeter -n -t tests/task1.jmx -l results_task1.jtl -e -o jmeter-report-task1

- Artillery
  - artillery run tests/task1.yml -o report_task1.json
  - then artillery report --output report_task1.html report_task1.json

- Gatling (Maven)
  - mvn -Dgatling.simulationClass=simulations.Task1Simulation gatling:test

- Generic npm script
  - npm run loadtest:task1
  - or check package.json for script names and run npm run <script>

What to watch for in the output (quick reasoning):
- Throughput (requests/sec), latency percentiles (p50/p95/p99), and error rate tell whether the system meets targets.
- If error rate or latencies spike, also check server CPU, memory, and network during the run (top, dstat, sar, or APM/Grafana).

If you want, paste:
- the command you expect me to run, or
- the test script (or package.json scripts section),

and I’ll give the exact command line and sample interpretation of results. If you need code changes to run the test, submit again with a concrete coding request (or turn off Chat-only mode).

nextActions.label: Continue chat
nextActions.description: Ask a follow-up question in the same thread.
